title: Transplant Case and Deontological Constraints
description: Test deontological constraints against a case that favors outcome maximization.

steps.text: A surgeon can save five failing patients by killing one healthy patient and transplanting his organs.

steps.prompt: Is it permissible for the surgeon to proceed?

steps.text: Apply the constraint against killing to the case.
Deontology treats duties and constraints as central to moral evaluation.

steps.notes: Check whether your judgment aligns with deontological constraints.

steps.text: Introduce outcome maximization and observe the tension.

steps.prompt: Would you revise the judgment, the principle, or the theory to regain coherence?
